originalTitle: Weather warnings as tropical depression develops along US Gulf coast
summary: Depression set to become tropical storm later on Monday as separate storm strengthens off Mexico’s Pacific coast
content: Tropical Storm Bertha developed along the US Gulf coast on Monday evening, bringing the threat of heavy rain and storm surge flooding as Tropical Storm Fausto strengthened off Mexico’s south-west Pacific coast. The center of Bertha was located about 110 miles (180 km) south of Panama City, Florida. Maximum sustained winds were around 40 mph, according to an evening advisory from the Miami-based National Hurricane Center. The slow-moving storm has been drifting erratically, but is expected to stay near or along the northern Gulf coast early this week, bringing heavy rainfall to areas of northern Florida, Alabama, Mississippi and Louisiana as it pushes westward toward Texas. Forecasters said some additional strengthening is expected in the coming hours. Bertha is the second tropical storm of the Atlantic hurricane season and is expected to make landfall in south-east Louisiana on Wednesday. Tropical Storm Arthur last month brought heavy rainfall to the south-eastern United States. A storm surge watch was in effect from the Alabama-Florida border westward to the mouth of the Mississippi River in Louisiana. A tropical storm watch, meanwhile, extended from the Mississippi-Alabama border to the line separating Louisiana’s Jefferson and Plaquemines parishes, the NHC added. The NHC said the storm surge watch indicated a risk of life-threatening coastal flooding from rising water moving inland from the shoreline within the next 48 hours. Combined with high tide, storm surge could flood normally dry coastal areas along parts of the northern Gulf coast. Water levels could rise by 2-4ft (60-120cm) from the mouth of the Mississippi River to the Alabama-Florida border, with 1-3ft of surge possible across portions of coastal Louisiana and Florida, including Lake Pontchartrain. The depression was also expected to produce widespread rainfall totals of 2-4in, with isolated amounts of up to 8in through Friday from western Florida to the middle Texas coast. The heavy rain could trigger flash flooding in some areas, while an isolated tornado or two was possible across Florida’s Big Bend region. Meanwhile, Tropical Storm Fausto continued to strengthen over the eastern Pacific. As of Monday morning, the storm was moving west-northwest at about 13mph with maximum sustained winds of 65mph. It is forecast to become a hurricane later Monday, with additional strengthening expected over the next few days. Although no coastal watches or warnings were in effect for Fausto, the NHC said swells generated by the storm were expected to reach the west coast of the Baja California Peninsula on Wednesday and persist for several days. The swells were likely to create life-threatening surf and rip current conditions. Fausto is forecast to continue tracking west-northwest through Wednesday.
chineseTitle: 热带低气压沿美国墨西哥湾沿岸发展的天气警告
chineseSummary: 随着墨西哥太平洋沿岸另一场风暴加强，低气压将于周一晚些时候转变为热带风暴
chineseContent: 周一晚间，热带风暴伯莎在美国墨西哥湾沿岸发展，随着热带风暴福斯托在墨西哥西南太平洋沿岸加强，带来了暴雨和风暴潮洪水的威胁。伯莎中心位于佛罗里达州巴拿马城以南约 180 公里（110 英里）处。根据迈阿密国家飓风中心晚间发布的报告，最大持续风速约为 40 英里/小时。这场缓慢移动的风暴一直不稳定地漂移，但预计本周初将停留在墨西哥湾北部海岸附近或沿线，向西推向德克萨斯州，给佛罗里达州北部、阿拉巴马州、密西西比州和路易斯安那州地区带来强降雨。预报员表示，预计未来几个小时内还会出现进一步的走强。伯莎是大西洋飓风季节的第二场热带风暴，预计将于周三在路易斯安那州东南部登陆。上个月，热带风暴亚瑟给美国东南部带来了强降雨。从阿拉巴马州和佛罗里达州边境向西到路易斯安那州密西西比河河口，风暴潮监视已经生效。国家飓风中心补充说，与此同时，热带风暴监视范围从密西西比州和阿拉巴马州边境延伸到路易斯安那州杰斐逊教区和普拉克明教区的分隔线。 NHC 表示，风暴潮监测表明，未来 48 小时内，上升的海水从海岸线向内陆移动，可能会引发危及生命的沿海洪水。加上涨潮，风暴潮可能会淹没墨西哥湾北部沿岸部分地区通常干燥的沿海地区。从密西西比河河口到阿拉巴马州与佛罗里达州边境的水位可能会上涨2-4英尺（60-120厘米），路易斯安那州和佛罗里达州沿海部分地区（包括庞恰特雷恩湖）的水位可能会上涨1-3英尺。该部门
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
